23.5.1 Main Route Table

hcl Main Route Table Management 
# Managing the default/main route table
resource "aws_default_route_table" "main" {
  default_route_table_id = aws_vpc.main.default_route_table_id
  
  # Local route is automatically included
  # Do NOT add 0.0.0.0/0 route to main table for security
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-main-rt"
  }
}

# Subnets without explicit association use the main route table
# Best practice: Explicitly associate all subnets

23.5.2 Custom Route Tables

hcl Complete Route Table Setup 
# Public route table
resource "aws_route_table" "public" {
  vpc_id = aws_vpc.main.id
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-public-rt"
  }
}

# Routes can be inline or separate resources
resource "aws_route" "public_internet" {
  route_table_id         = aws_route_table.public.id
  destination_cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"
  gateway_id             = aws_internet_gateway.main.id
}

# Private route table with NAT
resource "aws_route_table" "private" {
  vpc_id = aws_vpc.main.id
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-private-rt"
  }
}

resource "aws_route" "private_nat" {
  route_table_id         = aws_route_table.private.id
  destination_cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"
  nat_gateway_id         = aws_nat_gateway.main.id
}

23.5.3 Route Table Associations

hcl Subnet Associations with for_each 
# Using for_each for cleaner associations
resource "aws_route_table_association" "public" {
  for_each = { for idx, subnet in aws_subnet.public : idx => subnet }
  
  subnet_id      = each.value.id
  route_table_id = aws_route_table.public.id
}

resource "aws_route_table_association" "private" {
  for_each = { for idx, subnet in aws_subnet.private : idx => subnet }
  
  subnet_id      = each.value.id
  route_table_id = aws_route_table.private.id
}

# Gateway route table association (for Transit Gateway)
resource "aws_main_route_table_association" "main" {
  vpc_id         = aws_vpc.main.id
  route_table_id = aws_route_table.private.id
}

23.5.4 Route Propagation

hcl VPN Route Propagation 
# Enable route propagation from VPN Gateway
resource "aws_vpn_gateway_route_propagation" "private" {
  vpn_gateway_id = aws_vpn_gateway.main.id
  route_table_id = aws_route_table.private.id
}

# Routes learned from VPN appear automatically in route table
# Useful for dynamic routing with on-premises networks
23.6

Security Groups (aws_security_group)

Security Groups act as virtual firewalls for EC2 instances, controlling inbound and outbound traffic at the instance level. They are stateful - return traffic is automatically allowed.

23.6.1 Ingress Rules

hcl Security Group with Ingress Rules 
# Web server security group
resource "aws_security_group" "web" {
  name        = "${var.project}-web-sg"
  description = "Security group for web servers"
  vpc_id      = aws_vpc.main.id
  
  # Inline ingress rules
  ingress {
    description = "HTTP from anywhere"
    from_port   = 80
    to_port     = 80
    protocol    = "tcp"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }
  
  ingress {
    description = "HTTPS from anywhere"
    from_port   = 443
    to_port     = 443
    protocol    = "tcp"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }
  
  ingress {
    description = "SSH from bastion only"
    from_port   = 22
    to_port     = 22
    protocol    = "tcp"
    security_groups = [aws_security_group.bastion.id]
  }
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-web-sg"
  }
}

23.6.2 Egress Rules

hcl Egress Rule Configuration 
# Security group with explicit egress
resource "aws_security_group" "web" {
  name        = "${var.project}-web-sg"
  vpc_id      = aws_vpc.main.id
  
  # Allow all outbound traffic (common pattern)
  egress {
    description = "Allow all outbound"
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"  # All protocols
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }
  
  # Or restrict egress (more secure)
  egress {
    description = "HTTPS to internet"
    from_port   = 443
    to_port     = 443
    protocol    = "tcp"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }
  
  egress {
    description = "Database access"
    from_port   = 5432
    to_port     = 5432
    protocol    = "tcp"
    security_groups = [aws_security_group.database.id]
  }
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-web-sg"
  }
}
⚠️

Default Egress Behavior

If you specify ANY egress rules, you must explicitly allow all desired outbound traffic. Terraform removes the default "allow all" egress rule when you define custom egress rules.

23.6.3 Security Group Rules (Separate Resource)

hcl Separate Rule Resources 
# Security group without inline rules
resource "aws_security_group" "app" {
  name        = "${var.project}-app-sg"
  description = "Application security group"
  vpc_id      = aws_vpc.main.id
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-app-sg"
  }
}

# Separate ingress rules
resource "aws_security_group_rule" "app_https_in" {
  type              = "ingress"
  security_group_id = aws_security_group.app.id
  
  from_port   = 443
  to_port     = 443
  protocol    = "tcp"
  cidr_blocks = ["10.0.0.0/16"]
  description = "HTTPS from VPC"
}

resource "aws_security_group_rule" "app_from_alb" {
  type              = "ingress"
  security_group_id = aws_security_group.app.id
  
  from_port                = 8080
  to_port                  = 8080
  protocol                 = "tcp"
  source_security_group_id = aws_security_group.alb.id
  description              = "Traffic from ALB"
}

# Separate egress rules
resource "aws_security_group_rule" "app_all_out" {
  type              = "egress"
  security_group_id = aws_security_group.app.id
  
  from_port   = 0
  to_port     = 0
  protocol    = "-1"
  cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  description = "Allow all outbound"
}
💡 Inline vs Separate Rules

Use inline rules when security group is self-contained

Use separate rules when:

  • Rules are managed by different teams/modules
  • Circular references between security groups
  • Dynamic rules based on variables

⚠️ Do NOT mix inline and separate rules in the same security group!

23.6.4 Self-Referencing Rules

hcl Self-Referencing Security Group 
# Cluster security group (nodes can talk to each other)
resource "aws_security_group" "cluster" {
  name        = "${var.project}-cluster-sg"
  vpc_id      = aws_vpc.main.id
  
  # Allow all traffic within the cluster
  ingress {
    from_port = 0
    to_port   = 0
    protocol  = "-1"
    self      = true  # References itself
    description = "Allow all traffic within cluster"
  }
  
  egress {
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-cluster-sg"
  }
}

23.6.5 Security Group Chaining

ascii-diagram Security Group Chaining Pattern 
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    SECURITY GROUP CHAINING                           │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│   Internet                                                           │
│      │                                                               │
│      ▼                                                               │
│   ┌───────────────────────┐                                          │
│   │   ALB Security Group  │  ← Port 443 from 0.0.0.0/0              │
│   │   (alb-sg)            │                                          │
│   └───────────┬───────────┘                                          │
│               │ Port 8080                                            │
│               ▼                                                      │
│   ┌───────────────────────┐                                          │
│   │   App Security Group  │  ← Port 8080 from alb-sg                │
│   │   (app-sg)            │                                          │
│   └───────────┬───────────┘                                          │
│               │ Port 5432                                            │
│               ▼                                                      │
│   ┌───────────────────────┐                                          │
│   │   DB Security Group   │  ← Port 5432 from app-sg                │
│   │   (db-sg)             │                                          │
│   └───────────────────────┘                                          │
│                                                                      │
│   Reference security groups instead of CIDR blocks for:             │
│   ✓ Layer isolation                                                 │
│   ✓ Dynamic scaling (new instances automatically allowed)           │
│   ✓ Cleaner rule management                                         │
│                                                                      │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📝 EXAM TIP

Security Groups - Key points:

  • Stateful: Return traffic automatically allowed
  • Default deny: All inbound blocked by default
  • Only allow: No explicit deny rules possible
  • Instance level: Applied to ENI (network interface)
  • Reference SGs: Use security group IDs for dynamic membership
23.7

Network ACLs (aws_network_acl)

Network ACLs (NACLs) provide stateless, subnet-level traffic control. They serve as a secondary layer of defense alongside Security Groups.

23.7.1 Stateless Rules

Feature Security Group Network ACL
Level Instance (ENI) Subnet
Stateful/less Stateful Stateless
Rules Allow only Allow and Deny
Evaluation All rules Numbered order
Return traffic Automatic Must explicitly allow

23.7.2 Rule Numbering

hcl Network ACL with Numbered Rules 
# Custom Network ACL
resource "aws_network_acl" "public" {
  vpc_id     = aws_vpc.main.id
  subnet_ids = aws_subnet.public[*].id
  
  # Inbound rules (evaluated in order by rule_no)
  ingress {
    rule_no    = 100
    protocol   = "tcp"
    action     = "allow"
    cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"
    from_port  = 80
    to_port    = 80
  }
  
  ingress {
    rule_no    = 110
    protocol   = "tcp"
    action     = "allow"
    cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"
    from_port  = 443
    to_port    = 443
  }
  
  ingress {
    rule_no    = 120
    protocol   = "tcp"
    action     = "allow"
    cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"
    from_port  = 22
    to_port    = 22
  }
  
  # Ephemeral ports for return traffic (stateless!)
  ingress {
    rule_no    = 130
    protocol   = "tcp"
    action     = "allow"
    cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"
    from_port  = 1024
    to_port    = 65535
  }
  
  # Outbound rules
  egress {
    rule_no    = 100
    protocol   = "tcp"
    action     = "allow"
    cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"
    from_port  = 80
    to_port    = 80
  }
  
  egress {
    rule_no    = 110
    protocol   = "tcp"
    action     = "allow"
    cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"
    from_port  = 443
    to_port    = 443
  }
  
  # Ephemeral ports for responses
  egress {
    rule_no    = 120
    protocol   = "tcp"
    action     = "allow"
    cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"
    from_port  = 1024
    to_port    = 65535
  }
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-public-nacl"
  }
}

23.7.3 Default Network ACL

hcl Managing Default NACL 
# Manage the default NACL
resource "aws_default_network_acl" "default" {
  default_network_acl_id = aws_vpc.main.default_network_acl_id
  
  # Default behavior: deny all if no rules
  # Add explicit allow-all to maintain default behavior
  
  ingress {
    rule_no    = 100
    protocol   = "-1"
    action     = "allow"
    cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"
    from_port  = 0
    to_port    = 0
  }
  
  egress {
    rule_no    = 100
    protocol   = "-1"
    action     = "allow"
    cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"
    from_port  = 0
    to_port    = 0
  }
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-default-nacl"
  }
}

# Note: Subnets not explicitly associated with a NACL
# use the default NACL automatically
23.8

VPC Peering (aws_vpc_peering_connection)

23.8.1 Requester and Accepter

hcl Same-Account VPC Peering 
# VPC Peering connection (requester)
resource "aws_vpc_peering_connection" "peer" {
  vpc_id        = aws_vpc.main.id      # Requester VPC
  peer_vpc_id   = aws_vpc.other.id     # Accepter VPC
  auto_accept   = true                  # Auto-accept (same account only)
  
  tags = {
    Name = "main-to-other-peering"
  }
}

# Update route tables for peering
resource "aws_route" "main_to_other" {
  route_table_id            = aws_route_table.main_private.id
  destination_cidr_block    = aws_vpc.other.cidr_block
  vpc_peering_connection_id = aws_vpc_peering_connection.peer.id
}

resource "aws_route" "other_to_main" {
  route_table_id            = aws_route_table.other_private.id
  destination_cidr_block    = aws_vpc.main.cidr_block
  vpc_peering_connection_id = aws_vpc_peering_connection.peer.id
}

23.8.2 Cross-Account Peering

hcl Cross-Account VPC Peering 
# In requester account
resource "aws_vpc_peering_connection" "cross_account" {
  vpc_id        = aws_vpc.main.id
  peer_vpc_id   = var.peer_vpc_id
  peer_owner_id = var.peer_account_id  # Other AWS account ID
  peer_region   = var.peer_region      # If cross-region
  
  # Cannot auto_accept for cross-account
  auto_accept = false
  
  tags = {
    Name = "cross-account-peering"
  }
}

# In accepter account (separate Terraform config)
resource "aws_vpc_peering_connection_accepter" "accepter" {
  provider = aws.peer_account
  
  vpc_peering_connection_id = var.peering_connection_id
  auto_accept               = true
  
  tags = {
    Name = "accepted-peering"
  }
}

23.8.3 Cross-Region Peering

hcl Cross-Region VPC Peering 
# Provider for another region
provider "aws" {
  alias  = "uswest"
  region = "us-west-2"
}

# VPC in us-west-2
resource "aws_vpc" "west" {
  provider   = aws.uswest
  cidr_block = "10.1.0.0/16"
}

# Cross-region peering
resource "aws_vpc_peering_connection" "cross_region" {
  vpc_id      = aws_vpc.main.id          # us-east-1
  peer_vpc_id = aws_vpc.west.id          # us-west-2
  peer_region = "us-west-2"
  
  auto_accept = false  # Requires accepter
  
  tags = {
    Name = "east-to-west-peering"
  }
}

# Accept in peer region
resource "aws_vpc_peering_connection_accepter" "west_accepter" {
  provider                  = aws.uswest
  vpc_peering_connection_id = aws_vpc_peering_connection.cross_region.id
  auto_accept               = true
}
💡 VPC Peering Limitations
  • No transitive routing: VPC A ↔ B ↔ C doesn't mean A ↔ C
  • No overlapping CIDRs: Peered VPCs cannot have conflicting IP ranges
  • Limited DNS: Private DNS doesn't work across peering by default
  • Consider Transit Gateway for complex multi-VPC architectures
23.9

Transit Gateway (aws_ec2_transit_gateway)

Transit Gateway is a network transit hub that connects VPCs, on-premises networks, and other AWS services through a central gateway.

23.9.1 Transit Gateway Attachments

hcl Transit Gateway with VPC Attachments 
# Create Transit Gateway
resource "aws_ec2_transit_gateway" "main" {
  description = "Main Transit Gateway"
  
  default_route_table_association = "enable"
  default_route_table_propagation = "enable"
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-tgw"
  }
}

# Attach VPCs to Transit Gateway
resource "aws_ec2_transit_gateway_vpc_attachment" "vpc_a" {
  transit_gateway_id = aws_ec2_transit_gateway.main.id
  vpc_id             = aws_vpc.vpc_a.id
  subnet_ids         = aws_subnet.vpc_a_private[*].id
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-tgw-attach-vpc-a"
  }
}

resource "aws_ec2_transit_gateway_vpc_attachment" "vpc_b" {
  transit_gateway_id = aws_ec2_transit_gateway.main.id
  vpc_id             = aws_vpc.vpc_b.id
  subnet_ids         = aws_subnet.vpc_b_private[*].id
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-tgw-attach-vpc-b"
  }
}

23.9.2 Transit Gateway Route Tables

hcl VPC Routes to Transit Gateway 
# Route from VPC A to VPC B via Transit Gateway
resource "aws_route" "vpc_a_to_vpc_b" {
  route_table_id         = aws_route_table.vpc_a_private.id
  destination_cidr_block = aws_vpc.vpc_b.cidr_block
  transit_gateway_id     = aws_ec2_transit_gateway.main.id
  
  depends_on = [aws_ec2_transit_gateway_vpc_attachment.vpc_a]
}

resource "aws_route" "vpc_b_to_vpc_a" {
  route_table_id         = aws_route_table.vpc_b_private.id
  destination_cidr_block = aws_vpc.vpc_a.cidr_block
  transit_gateway_id     = aws_ec2_transit_gateway.main.id
  
  depends_on = [aws_ec2_transit_gateway_vpc_attachment.vpc_b]
}

23.9.3 Multi-Account Patterns

ascii-diagram Hub-and-Spoke Architecture 
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  TRANSIT GATEWAY HUB-AND-SPOKE                       │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│              Shared Services VPC                                     │
│              (DNS, AD, NTP)                                          │
│                    │                                                 │
│                    ▼                                                 │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│   │                   TRANSIT GATEWAY                           │    │
│   │              (Central Network Hub)                          │    │
│   └──────┬───────────────┬───────────────┬───────────────┬──────┘    │
│          │               │               │               │           │
│          ▼               ▼               ▼               ▼           │
│     ┌─────────┐   ┌─────────┐   ┌─────────┐   ┌─────────┐           │
│     │ Dev VPC │   │ Prod VPC│   │ Test VPC│   │ On-Prem │           │
│     │ Account │   │ Account │   │ Account │   │  (VPN)  │           │
│     └─────────┘   └─────────┘   └─────────┘   └─────────┘           │
│                                                                      │
│   Benefits:                                                          │
│   ✓ Transitive routing (all VPCs can reach each other)              │
│   ✓ Central management                                               │
│   ✓ Scales to thousands of VPCs                                      │
│   ✓ Supports VPN and Direct Connect                                  │
│                                                                      │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
23.10

VPC Endpoints

VPC Endpoints allow private connections to AWS services without requiring an Internet Gateway, NAT device, VPN, or AWS Direct Connect.

23.10.1 Gateway Endpoints (S3, DynamoDB)

hcl S3 Gateway Endpoint 
# S3 Gateway Endpoint (free, no hourly charges)
resource "aws_vpc_endpoint" "s3" {
  vpc_id       = aws_vpc.main.id
  service_name = "com.amazonaws.${var.region}.s3"
  
  vpc_endpoint_type = "Gateway"
  
  # Associate with route tables
  route_table_ids = concat(
    aws_route_table.private[*].id,
    [aws_route_table.public.id]
  )
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-s3-endpoint"
  }
}

# DynamoDB Gateway Endpoint
resource "aws_vpc_endpoint" "dynamodb" {
  vpc_id       = aws_vpc.main.id
  service_name = "com.amazonaws.${var.region}.dynamodb"
  
  vpc_endpoint_type = "Gateway"
  route_table_ids   = aws_route_table.private[*].id
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-dynamodb-endpoint"
  }
}
💡 Gateway Endpoints
  • Free: No hourly charges or data processing fees
  • Route-based: Added to route tables
  • Only S3 and DynamoDB: These are the only Gateway endpoints

23.10.2 Interface Endpoints

hcl Interface Endpoints for AWS Services 
# Security group for VPC endpoints
resource "aws_security_group" "vpc_endpoints" {
  name        = "${var.project}-vpce-sg"
  description = "Security group for VPC endpoints"
  vpc_id      = aws_vpc.main.id
  
  ingress {
    description = "HTTPS from VPC"
    from_port   = 443
    to_port     = 443
    protocol    = "tcp"
    cidr_blocks = [aws_vpc.main.cidr_block]
  }
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-vpce-sg"
  }
}

# EC2 Interface Endpoint
resource "aws_vpc_endpoint" "ec2" {
  vpc_id             = aws_vpc.main.id
  service_name       = "com.amazonaws.${var.region}.ec2"
  vpc_endpoint_type  = "Interface"
  
  subnet_ids         = aws_subnet.private[*].id
  security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.vpc_endpoints.id]
  
  private_dns_enabled = true  # Requires enable_dns_hostnames on VPC
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-ec2-endpoint"
  }
}

# SSM Interface Endpoints (for Session Manager)
resource "aws_vpc_endpoint" "ssm" {
  vpc_id              = aws_vpc.main.id
  service_name        = "com.amazonaws.${var.region}.ssm"
  vpc_endpoint_type   = "Interface"
  subnet_ids          = aws_subnet.private[*].id
  security_group_ids  = [aws_security_group.vpc_endpoints.id]
  private_dns_enabled = true
  
  tags = { Name = "${var.project}-ssm-endpoint" }
}

resource "aws_vpc_endpoint" "ssmmessages" {
  vpc_id              = aws_vpc.main.id
  service_name        = "com.amazonaws.${var.region}.ssmmessages"
  vpc_endpoint_type   = "Interface"
  subnet_ids          = aws_subnet.private[*].id
  security_group_ids  = [aws_security_group.vpc_endpoints.id]
  private_dns_enabled = true
  
  tags = { Name = "${var.project}-ssmmessages-endpoint" }
}

resource "aws_vpc_endpoint" "ec2messages" {
  vpc_id              = aws_vpc.main.id
  service_name        = "com.amazonaws.${var.region}.ec2messages"
  vpc_endpoint_type   = "Interface"
  subnet_ids          = aws_subnet.private[*].id
  security_group_ids  = [aws_security_group.vpc_endpoints.id]
  private_dns_enabled = true
  
  tags = { Name = "${var.project}-ec2messages-endpoint" }
}

23.10.3 PrivateLink

hcl PrivateLink for Custom Services 
# Consumer side: Connect to a PrivateLink service
resource "aws_vpc_endpoint" "privatelink_service" {
  vpc_id             = aws_vpc.consumer.id
  service_name       = var.endpoint_service_name  # Service provider gives this
  vpc_endpoint_type  = "Interface"
  
  subnet_ids         = aws_subnet.consumer_private[*].id
  security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.privatelink.id]
  
  tags = {
    Name = "privatelink-to-provider"
  }
}

# Provider side: Expose a service via PrivateLink
resource "aws_vpc_endpoint_service" "my_service" {
  acceptance_required        = true
  network_load_balancer_arns = [aws_lb.nlb.arn]
  
  allowed_principals = [
    "arn:aws:iam::${var.consumer_account_id}:root"
  ]
  
  tags = {
    Name = "my-privatelink-service"
  }
}
23.11

Elastic IP (aws_eip)

23.11.1 VPC Domain

hcl Elastic IP Allocation 
# Allocate Elastic IP for VPC
resource "aws_eip" "web" {
  domain = "vpc"  # Required for VPC (replaces deprecated 'vpc = true')
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-web-eip"
  }
  
  # Ensure IGW exists first
  depends_on = [aws_internet_gateway.main]
}

# Or bring your own IP (BYOIP)
resource "aws_eip" "byoip" {
  domain           = "vpc"
  public_ipv4_pool = "ipv4pool-ec2-xxxxx"  # Your BYOIP pool
}

23.11.2 EIP Association

hcl EIP Association Methods 
# Method 1: Direct association in EIP resource
resource "aws_eip" "web" {
  domain   = "vpc"
  instance = aws_instance.web.id
}

# Method 2: Separate association resource (recommended)
resource "aws_eip" "web" {
  domain = "vpc"
}

resource "aws_eip_association" "web" {
  instance_id   = aws_instance.web.id
  allocation_id = aws_eip.web.id
}

# Method 3: Associate with network interface
resource "aws_eip_association" "eni" {
  network_interface_id = aws_network_interface.web.id
  allocation_id        = aws_eip.web.id
}
💰

EIP Costs

Elastic IPs are free when attached to running instances. You are charged for EIPs that are allocated but not associated, or associated with stopped instances.

23.12

VPN Configuration

23.12.1 Customer Gateway

hcl Customer Gateway for On-Premises 
# Customer Gateway (on-premises router)
resource "aws_customer_gateway" "onprem" {
  bgp_asn    = 65000                   # On-premises ASN
  ip_address = var.onprem_public_ip    # On-premises router public IP
  type       = "ipsec.1"
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-customer-gateway"
  }
}

23.12.2 VPN Gateway

hcl Virtual Private Gateway 
# Virtual Private Gateway (AWS side)
resource "aws_vpn_gateway" "main" {
  vpc_id = aws_vpc.main.id
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-vpn-gateway"
  }
}

# Enable route propagation
resource "aws_vpn_gateway_route_propagation" "private" {
  vpn_gateway_id = aws_vpn_gateway.main.id
  route_table_id = aws_route_table.private.id
}

23.12.3 Site-to-Site VPN Connection

hcl Complete VPN Connection 
# Site-to-Site VPN Connection
resource "aws_vpn_connection" "main" {
  vpn_gateway_id        = aws_vpn_gateway.main.id
  customer_gateway_id   = aws_customer_gateway.onprem.id
  type                  = "ipsec.1"
  static_routes_only    = false  # Use BGP for dynamic routing
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-vpn-connection"
  }
}

# If using static routes instead of BGP
resource "aws_vpn_connection_route" "office_network" {
  count = var.static_routes_only ? 1 : 0
  
  destination_cidr_block = var.onprem_cidr
  vpn_connection_id      = aws_vpn_connection.main.id
}

# Output VPN configuration for on-premises router
output "vpn_tunnel1_address" {
  value = aws_vpn_connection.main.tunnel1_address
}

output "vpn_tunnel1_preshared_key" {
  value     = aws_vpn_connection.main.tunnel1_preshared_key
  sensitive = true
}

output "vpn_tunnel2_address" {
  value = aws_vpn_connection.main.tunnel2_address
}

Chapter 23 Summary

Key Takeaways

  • VPC: Foundation of AWS networking with CIDR blocks, DNS settings, and tenancy
  • Subnets: Public (with IGW route) vs Private (NAT Gateway for outbound)
  • Internet Gateway: One per VPC, enables internet connectivity for public subnets
  • NAT Gateway: Managed service for private subnet outbound access, use per-AZ for HA
  • Security Groups: Stateful, instance-level, allow-only rules
  • NACLs: Stateless, subnet-level, numbered rules with allow/deny
  • VPC Peering: Direct VPC-to-VPC connection, no transitive routing
  • Transit Gateway: Hub-and-spoke for multi-VPC architectures
  • VPC Endpoints: Gateway (S3/DynamoDB, free) vs Interface (other services, hourly charge)
📝 EXAM TIP

AWS Networking Quick Reference:

  • Security Groups: Stateful, no deny rules, evaluated together
  • NACLs: Stateless, allow/deny, evaluated in order
  • NAT Gateway: Must be in public subnet with EIP
  • Gateway Endpoints: Only S3 and DynamoDB, free
  • enable_dns_hostnames = true for private DNS to work
Chapter 24

AWS Compute Resources

EC2 Instances, Auto Scaling, and Load Balancers with Terraform

8 Sections
Part 8: AWS Resources
55 Minutes

Learning Objectives

💻

EC2 Instances

Create and configure EC2 instances with AMIs, user data, and IAM roles

📈

Auto Scaling

Implement Auto Scaling Groups with scaling policies and lifecycle hooks

⚖️

Load Balancing

Configure ALB and NLB with target groups, listeners, and health checks

💾

EBS Volumes

Manage block storage with various volume types and encryption

24.1

EC2 Instances (aws_instance)

24.1.1 AMI Selection

hcl Basic EC2 Instance with AMI 
# Data source to get latest Amazon Linux 2023 AMI
data "aws_ami" "amazon_linux" {
  most_recent = true
  owners      = ["amazon"]
  
  filter {
    name   = "name"
    values = ["al2023-ami-*-x86_64"]
  }
  
  filter {
    name   = "virtualization-type"
    values = ["hvm"]
  }
}

# Basic EC2 instance
resource "aws_instance" "web" {
  ami           = data.aws_ami.amazon_linux.id
  instance_type = var.instance_type
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-web-server"
  }
}

24.1.2-5 Instance Type, Key Pair, Security Groups, Subnet

hcl Complete EC2 Instance Configuration 
# Well-configured EC2 instance
resource "aws_instance" "web" {
  ami           = data.aws_ami.amazon_linux.id
  instance_type = "t3.micro"
  
  # Networking
  subnet_id                   = aws_subnet.public[0].id
  vpc_security_group_ids      = [aws_security_group.web.id]
  associate_public_ip_address = true
  
  # SSH access
  key_name = aws_key_pair.deployer.key_name
  
  # Instance profile for AWS API access
  iam_instance_profile = aws_iam_instance_profile.web.name
  
  # Monitoring
  monitoring = true  # Detailed monitoring (1-minute)
  
  # Termination protection
  disable_api_termination = var.environment == "prod"
  
  # Stop vs Terminate on shutdown
  instance_initiated_shutdown_behavior = "stop"
  
  # Credit specification for burstable instances
  credit_specification {
    cpu_credits = "standard"  # or "unlimited"
  }
  
  tags = {
    Name        = "${var.project}-web-server"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

24.1.6 User Data

hcl User Data Scripts 
# Method 1: Inline user data
resource "aws_instance" "web" {
  ami           = data.aws_ami.amazon_linux.id
  instance_type = "t3.micro"
  
  user_data = <<-EOF
    #!/bin/bash
    yum update -y
    yum install -y httpd
    systemctl start httpd
    systemctl enable httpd
    echo "<h1>Hello from $(hostname)</h1>" > /var/www/html/index.html
  EOF
  
  # Force replacement when user_data changes
  user_data_replace_on_change = true
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-web"
  }
}

# Method 2: Base64 encoded
resource "aws_instance" "web" {
  ami           = data.aws_ami.amazon_linux.id
  instance_type = "t3.micro"
  
  user_data_base64 = base64encode(templatefile("${path.module}/userdata.sh", {
    environment = var.environment
    app_version = var.app_version
  }))
}

# Method 3: From file
resource "aws_instance" "web" {
  ami           = data.aws_ami.amazon_linux.id
  instance_type = "t3.micro"
  
  user_data = file("${path.module}/scripts/bootstrap.sh")
}
⚠️

User Data Behavior

User data only runs on first boot by default. Changing user data will replace the instance if user_data_replace_on_change = true. User data logs: /var/log/cloud-init-output.log

24.1.7 IAM Instance Profile

hcl Instance Profile Configuration 
# IAM role for EC2
resource "aws_iam_role" "web" {
  name = "${var.project}-web-role"
  
  assume_role_policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Action = "sts:AssumeRole"
      Effect = "Allow"
      Principal = {
        Service = "ec2.amazonaws.com"
      }
    }]
  })
}

# Attach managed policy
resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "ssm" {
  role       = aws_iam_role.web.name
  policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore"
}

# Instance profile (wraps the role)
resource "aws_iam_instance_profile" "web" {
  name = "${var.project}-web-profile"
  role = aws_iam_role.web.name
}

# Use in EC2 instance
resource "aws_instance" "web" {
  # ...
  iam_instance_profile = aws_iam_instance_profile.web.name
}

24.1.8 Root Block Device

hcl Root Volume Configuration 
resource "aws_instance" "web" {
  ami           = data.aws_ami.amazon_linux.id
  instance_type = "t3.micro"
  
  root_block_device {
    volume_type           = "gp3"
    volume_size           = 30          # GB
    iops                  = 3000        # For gp3/io1/io2
    throughput            = 125         # MB/s, gp3 only
    encrypted             = true
    kms_key_id            = aws_kms_key.ebs.arn
    delete_on_termination = true
    
    tags = {
      Name = "${var.project}-web-root"
    }
  }
  
  # Additional EBS volumes
  ebs_block_device {
    device_name           = "/dev/sdb"
    volume_type           = "gp3"
    volume_size           = 100
    encrypted             = true
    delete_on_termination = false
    
    tags = {
      Name = "${var.project}-web-data"
    }
  }
}

24.1.9 Tags

hcl Comprehensive Tagging 
resource "aws_instance" "web" {
  ami           = data.aws_ami.amazon_linux.id
  instance_type = "t3.micro"
  
  tags = merge(
    var.common_tags,
    {
      Name        = "${var.project}-${var.environment}-web"
      Role        = "webserver"
      Environment = var.environment
      ManagedBy   = "terraform"
      CostCenter  = var.cost_center
    }
  )
  
  # Propagate tags to volumes
  volume_tags = merge(
    var.common_tags,
    {
      Name      = "${var.project}-${var.environment}-web-volume"
      ManagedBy = "terraform"
    }
  )
}
24.2

AMI Management

24.2.1-2 aws_ami Data Source and Filtering

hcl AMI Data Source Examples 
# Latest Amazon Linux 2023
data "aws_ami" "amazon_linux_2023" {
  most_recent = true
  owners      = ["amazon"]
  
  filter {
    name   = "name"
    values = ["al2023-ami-*-x86_64"]
  }
}

# Latest Ubuntu 22.04 LTS
data "aws_ami" "ubuntu" {
  most_recent = true
  owners      = ["099720109477"]  # Canonical
  
  filter {
    name   = "name"
    values = ["ubuntu/images/hvm-ssd/ubuntu-jammy-22.04-amd64-server-*"]
  }
}

# Windows Server 2022
data "aws_ami" "windows_2022" {
  most_recent = true
  owners      = ["amazon"]
  
  filter {
    name   = "name"
    values = ["Windows_Server-2022-English-Full-Base-*"]
  }
}

# Custom AMI from your account
data "aws_ami" "custom" {
  most_recent = true
  owners      = ["self"]
  
  filter {
    name   = "name"
    values = ["my-golden-image-*"]
  }
  
  filter {
    name   = "tag:Environment"
    values = ["production"]
  }
}

24.2.3-4 Custom AMIs and AMI Copy

hcl Creating and Copying AMIs 
# Create AMI from instance
resource "aws_ami_from_instance" "golden_image" {
  name               = "golden-image-${formatdate("YYYYMMDD", timestamp())}"
  source_instance_id = aws_instance.template.id
  
  tags = {
    Name        = "Golden Image"
    Environment = "production"
  }
}

# Copy AMI to another region
resource "aws_ami_copy" "cross_region" {
  name              = "golden-image-us-west-2"
  source_ami_id     = aws_ami_from_instance.golden_image.id
  source_ami_region = "us-east-1"
  encrypted         = true
  kms_key_id        = aws_kms_key.ami.arn
  
  tags = {
    Name = "Golden Image - Cross Region"
  }
}
24.3

Key Pairs (aws_key_pair)

24.3.1-2 Creating and Managing Key Pairs

hcl SSH Key Pair Management 
# Method 1: Use existing public key
resource "aws_key_pair" "deployer" {
  key_name   = "${var.project}-deployer-key"
  public_key = file("~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub")
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-deployer-key"
  }
}

# Method 2: Generate key pair with TLS provider
resource "tls_private_key" "ssh" {
  algorithm = "RSA"
  rsa_bits  = 4096
}

resource "aws_key_pair" "generated" {
  key_name   = "${var.project}-generated-key"
  public_key = tls_private_key.ssh.public_key_openssh
}

# Store private key in Secrets Manager (optional)
resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret" "ssh_key" {
  name = "${var.project}/ssh-private-key"
}

resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret_version" "ssh_key" {
  secret_id     = aws_secretsmanager_secret.ssh_key.id
  secret_string = tls_private_key.ssh.private_key_pem
}

# Output the private key (be careful!)
output "private_key" {
  value     = tls_private_key.ssh.private_key_pem
  sensitive = true
}
24.4

EBS Volumes

24.4.1-2 aws_ebs_volume and Attachment

hcl EBS Volume Creation and Attachment 
# Create standalone EBS volume
resource "aws_ebs_volume" "data" {
  availability_zone = aws_instance.web.availability_zone
  size              = 100    # GB
  type              = "gp3"
  iops              = 3000
  throughput        = 125
  encrypted         = true
  kms_key_id        = aws_kms_key.ebs.arn
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-data-volume"
  }
}

# Attach volume to instance
resource "aws_volume_attachment" "data" {
  device_name = "/dev/sdf"
  volume_id   = aws_ebs_volume.data.id
  instance_id = aws_instance.web.id
}

24.4.3-4 Volume Types and Encryption

Type Use Case Size Max IOPS Max Throughput
gp3 General purpose (recommended) 1 GiB - 16 TiB 16,000 1,000 MiB/s
gp2 General purpose (legacy) 1 GiB - 16 TiB 16,000 250 MiB/s
io2 High-performance databases 4 GiB - 16 TiB 64,000 1,000 MiB/s
st1 Throughput-optimized HDD 125 GiB - 16 TiB 500 500 MiB/s
sc1 Cold HDD (infrequent access) 125 GiB - 16 TiB 250 250 MiB/s
24.5

Launch Templates (aws_launch_template)

24.5.1-3 Launch Templates with Spot Instances

hcl Complete Launch Template 
resource "aws_launch_template" "web" {
  name          = "${var.project}-web-template"
  image_id      = data.aws_ami.amazon_linux.id
  instance_type = "t3.micro"
  key_name      = aws_key_pair.deployer.key_name
  
  # Network
  vpc_security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.web.id]
  
  # IAM
  iam_instance_profile {
    name = aws_iam_instance_profile.web.name
  }
  
  # User data
  user_data = base64encode(file("${path.module}/userdata.sh"))
  
  # Block device mappings
  block_device_mappings {
    device_name = "/dev/xvda"
    ebs {
      volume_size           = 20
      volume_type           = "gp3"
      encrypted             = true
      delete_on_termination = true
    }
  }
  
  # Monitoring
  monitoring {
    enabled = true
  }
  
  # Metadata options (IMDSv2)
  metadata_options {
    http_endpoint               = "enabled"
    http_tokens                 = "required"  # Require IMDSv2
    http_put_response_hop_limit = 1
  }
  
  # Tag specifications
  tag_specifications {
    resource_type = "instance"
    tags = {
      Name = "${var.project}-web"
    }
  }
  
  tag_specifications {
    resource_type = "volume"
    tags = {
      Name = "${var.project}-web-volume"
    }
  }
  
  # Spot instance options
  instance_market_options {
    market_type = "spot"
    spot_options {
      max_price          = "0.03"
      spot_instance_type = "one-time"
    }
  }
  
  lifecycle {
    create_before_destroy = true
  }
}
24.6

Auto Scaling

EXAM TOPIC
Auto Scaling Group Integration: Auto Scaling Groups (ASG) automatically scale EC2 instances based on demand. The ASG uses a Launch Template and manages instance lifecycle including health checks, scaling policies, and target group registration.

24.6.1 aws_autoscaling_group Resource

hcl Complete Auto Scaling Group 
resource "aws_autoscaling_group" "web" {
  name                = "${var.project}-web-asg"
  vpc_zone_identifier = aws_subnet.private[*].id
  
  # Capacity settings
  min_size         = 2
  max_size         = 10
  desired_capacity = 2
  
  # Launch template
  launch_template {
    id      = aws_launch_template.web.id
    version = "$Latest"  # or "$Default" or specific version
  }
  
  # Load balancer integration
  target_group_arns = [aws_lb_target_group.web.arn]
  
  # Health checks
  health_check_type         = "ELB"  # "EC2" or "ELB"
  health_check_grace_period = 300    # seconds
  
  # Instance refresh
  instance_refresh {
    strategy = "Rolling"
    preferences {
      min_healthy_percentage = 50
      instance_warmup        = 300
    }
  }
  
  # Instance protection
  protect_from_scale_in = false
  
  # Termination policies
  termination_policies = ["OldestInstance", "Default"]
  
  # Tags propagated to instances
  tag {
    key                 = "Name"
    value               = "${var.project}-web"
    propagate_at_launch = true
  }
  
  tag {
    key                 = "Environment"
    value               = var.environment
    propagate_at_launch = true
  }
  
  lifecycle {
    create_before_destroy = true
    ignore_changes        = [desired_capacity]  # Let ASG manage
  }
}

24.6.2-3 Scaling Policies (Target Tracking and Step)

hcl Auto Scaling Policies 
# Target tracking policy (recommended)
resource "aws_autoscaling_policy" "cpu" {
  name                   = "${var.project}-cpu-policy"
  autoscaling_group_name = aws_autoscaling_group.web.name
  policy_type            = "TargetTrackingScaling"
  
  target_tracking_configuration {
    predefined_metric_specification {
      predefined_metric_type = "ASGAverageCPUUtilization"
    }
    target_value     = 70.0
    disable_scale_in = false
  }
}

# Target tracking - ALB request count
resource "aws_autoscaling_policy" "alb_requests" {
  name                   = "${var.project}-alb-requests-policy"
  autoscaling_group_name = aws_autoscaling_group.web.name
  policy_type            = "TargetTrackingScaling"
  
  target_tracking_configuration {
    predefined_metric_specification {
      predefined_metric_type = "ALBRequestCountPerTarget"
      resource_label = "${aws_lb.web.arn_suffix}/${aws_lb_target_group.web.arn_suffix}"
    }
    target_value = 1000
  }
}

# Step scaling policy
resource "aws_autoscaling_policy" "scale_up" {
  name                   = "${var.project}-scale-up"
  autoscaling_group_name = aws_autoscaling_group.web.name
  adjustment_type        = "ChangeInCapacity"
  policy_type            = "StepScaling"
  
  step_adjustment {
    scaling_adjustment          = 1
    metric_interval_lower_bound = 0
    metric_interval_upper_bound = 20
  }
  
  step_adjustment {
    scaling_adjustment          = 2
    metric_interval_lower_bound = 20
  }
}

# CloudWatch alarm for step scaling
resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "high_cpu" {
  alarm_name          = "${var.project}-high-cpu"
  comparison_operator = "GreaterThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = 2
  metric_name         = "CPUUtilization"
  namespace           = "AWS/EC2"
  period              = 120
  statistic           = "Average"
  threshold           = 80
  alarm_actions       = [aws_autoscaling_policy.scale_up.arn]
  
  dimensions = {
    AutoScalingGroupName = aws_autoscaling_group.web.name
  }
}

24.6.4 Scheduled Scaling

hcl Scheduled Scaling Actions 
# Scale up during business hours
resource "aws_autoscaling_schedule" "scale_up_morning" {
  scheduled_action_name  = "scale-up-morning"
  min_size               = 4
  max_size               = 20
  desired_capacity       = 6
  recurrence             = "0 8 * * MON-FRI"  # 8 AM weekdays
  time_zone              = "America/New_York"
  autoscaling_group_name = aws_autoscaling_group.web.name
}

# Scale down after hours
resource "aws_autoscaling_schedule" "scale_down_evening" {
  scheduled_action_name  = "scale-down-evening"
  min_size               = 2
  max_size               = 10
  desired_capacity       = 2
  recurrence             = "0 20 * * MON-FRI"  # 8 PM weekdays
  time_zone              = "America/New_York"
  autoscaling_group_name = aws_autoscaling_group.web.name
}

24.6.5 Lifecycle Hooks

hcl ASG Lifecycle Hooks 
# Launch lifecycle hook
resource "aws_autoscaling_lifecycle_hook" "launch" {
  name                   = "${var.project}-launch-hook"
  autoscaling_group_name = aws_autoscaling_group.web.name
  lifecycle_transition   = "autoscaling:EC2_INSTANCE_LAUNCHING"
  heartbeat_timeout      = 300
  default_result         = "CONTINUE"  # or "ABANDON"
  
  notification_target_arn = aws_sns_topic.asg_notifications.arn
  role_arn                = aws_iam_role.lifecycle_hook.arn
}

# Terminate lifecycle hook
resource "aws_autoscaling_lifecycle_hook" "terminate" {
  name                   = "${var.project}-terminate-hook"
  autoscaling_group_name = aws_autoscaling_group.web.name
  lifecycle_transition   = "autoscaling:EC2_INSTANCE_TERMINATING"
  heartbeat_timeout      = 600
  default_result         = "CONTINUE"
}
24.7

Load Balancers

EXAM TOPIC
Load Balancer Types: ALB (Application) operates at Layer 7 (HTTP/HTTPS) with path-based routing. NLB (Network) operates at Layer 4 (TCP/UDP) with ultra-low latency. CLB (Classic) is legacy. All modern deployments should use ALB or NLB.

24.7.1 Application Load Balancer (ALB)

hcl Complete ALB Configuration 
# Application Load Balancer
resource "aws_lb" "web" {
  name               = "${var.project}-alb"
  internal           = false
  load_balancer_type = "application"
  security_groups    = [aws_security_group.alb.id]
  subnets            = aws_subnet.public[*].id
  
  enable_deletion_protection = var.environment == "prod"
  enable_http2               = true
  idle_timeout               = 60
  
  access_logs {
    bucket  = aws_s3_bucket.alb_logs.id
    prefix  = "alb"
    enabled = true
  }
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-alb"
  }
}

# Target Group
resource "aws_lb_target_group" "web" {
  name        = "${var.project}-tg"
  port        = 80
  protocol    = "HTTP"
  vpc_id      = aws_vpc.main.id
  target_type = "instance"  # or "ip" for Fargate
  
  health_check {
    enabled             = true
    healthy_threshold   = 2
    unhealthy_threshold = 2
    timeout             = 5
    interval            = 30
    path                = "/health"
    matcher             = "200-299"
  }
  
  stickiness {
    type            = "lb_cookie"
    cookie_duration = 86400
    enabled         = false
  }
  
  deregistration_delay = 30
}

# HTTP Listener (redirect to HTTPS)
resource "aws_lb_listener" "http" {
  load_balancer_arn = aws_lb.web.arn
  port              = 80
  protocol          = "HTTP"
  
  default_action {
    type = "redirect"
    redirect {
      port        = "443"
      protocol    = "HTTPS"
      status_code = "HTTP_301"
    }
  }
}

# HTTPS Listener
resource "aws_lb_listener" "https" {
  load_balancer_arn = aws_lb.web.arn
  port              = 443
  protocol          = "HTTPS"
  ssl_policy        = "ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS-1-2-2017-01"
  certificate_arn   = aws_acm_certificate.web.arn
  
  default_action {
    type             = "forward"
    target_group_arn = aws_lb_target_group.web.arn
  }
}

# Listener Rule (path-based routing)
resource "aws_lb_listener_rule" "api" {
  listener_arn = aws_lb_listener.https.arn
  priority     = 100
  
  action {
    type             = "forward"
    target_group_arn = aws_lb_target_group.api.arn
  }
  
  condition {
    path_pattern {
      values = ["/api/*"]
    }
  }
}

24.7.2 Network Load Balancer (NLB)

hcl NLB Configuration 
# Network Load Balancer
resource "aws_lb" "network" {
  name               = "${var.project}-nlb"
  internal           = false
  load_balancer_type = "network"
  subnets            = aws_subnet.public[*].id
  
  enable_cross_zone_load_balancing = true
  enable_deletion_protection       = var.environment == "prod"
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-nlb"
  }
}

# NLB Target Group
resource "aws_lb_target_group" "tcp" {
  name        = "${var.project}-tcp-tg"
  port        = 443
  protocol    = "TCP"
  vpc_id      = aws_vpc.main.id
  target_type = "instance"
  
  health_check {
    enabled             = true
    protocol            = "TCP"
    port                = "traffic-port"
    healthy_threshold   = 3
    unhealthy_threshold = 3
    interval            = 30
  }
}

# NLB Listener
resource "aws_lb_listener" "tcp" {
  load_balancer_arn = aws_lb.network.arn
  port              = 443
  protocol          = "TCP"
  
  default_action {
    type             = "forward"
    target_group_arn = aws_lb_target_group.tcp.arn
  }
}
24.8

Placement Groups

24.8.1-3 Cluster, Spread, and Partition Strategies

hcl Placement Group Strategies 
# Cluster - Low latency, high throughput (HPC)
resource "aws_placement_group" "cluster" {
  name     = "${var.project}-cluster"
  strategy = "cluster"
}

# Spread - High availability (max 7 per AZ)
resource "aws_placement_group" "spread" {
  name         = "${var.project}-spread"
  strategy     = "spread"
  spread_level = "rack"  # or "host"
}

# Partition - Large distributed workloads (Hadoop, Cassandra)
resource "aws_placement_group" "partition" {
  name            = "${var.project}-partition"
  strategy        = "partition"
  partition_count = 7
}

# Use in EC2 instance
resource "aws_instance" "hpc" {
  ami             = data.aws_ami.amazon_linux.id
  instance_type   = "c5n.18xlarge"
  placement_group = aws_placement_group.cluster.id
}
Strategy Use Case Limitations
Cluster HPC, low latency networking Single AZ, same instance type recommended
Spread Critical instances needing HA Max 7 instances per AZ
Partition HDFS, HBase, Cassandra Max 7 partitions per AZ

Chapter 24 Summary: AWS Compute Resources

Key Takeaways

  • EC2 Instances: Use aws_instance with AMI, instance type, key pair, security groups, subnet, user_data, and IAM instance profile
  • AMI Data Source: Use aws_ami data source with filters to find the latest AMI by owner, name pattern, and architecture
  • EBS Volumes: gp3 is the recommended general-purpose volume type; use io2 for high-performance databases
  • Launch Templates: Use instead of launch configurations for ASG; support versioning and mixed instance types
  • Auto Scaling: Configure min/max/desired capacity, health checks (EC2 or ELB), and scaling policies
  • Target Tracking: Recommended scaling policy type; automatically adjusts based on target metric value
  • ALB vs NLB: ALB for Layer 7 (HTTP path routing), NLB for Layer 4 (TCP/UDP, ultra-low latency)
  • Placement Groups: Cluster for HPC, Spread for HA (max 7/AZ), Partition for distributed systems
Chapter 25

AWS Storage Resources

S3 Buckets, Objects, EFS, and Backup Strategies with Terraform

6 Sections
Part 8: AWS Resources
45 Minutes

Learning Objectives

🪣

S3 Buckets

Create and configure S3 buckets with versioning, encryption, and lifecycle policies

📁

S3 Objects

Upload and manage objects with storage classes and metadata

🌐

Static Websites

Host static websites on S3 with custom domains and CloudFront

📂

EFS

Configure Elastic File System for shared storage across instances

25.1

S3 Buckets (aws_s3_bucket)

EXAM TOPIC
AWS Provider 4.0+ Changes: Since AWS provider 4.0, S3 bucket configurations are split into separate resources: aws_s3_bucket_versioning, aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration, aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration, etc. The inline arguments in aws_s3_bucket are deprecated.

25.1.1-2 Bucket Creation and Naming

hcl S3 Bucket Creation 
# S3 bucket (basic)
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "data" {
  bucket = "${var.project}-data-${random_id.suffix.hex}"
  
  tags = {
    Name        = "${var.project}-data"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

# Random suffix for unique bucket names
resource "random_id" "suffix" {
  byte_length = 4
}

# Block public access (recommended)
resource "aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block" "data" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.data.id
  
  block_public_acls       = true
  block_public_policy     = true
  ignore_public_acls      = true
  restrict_public_buckets = true
}

25.1.3 Versioning

hcl S3 Bucket Versioning 
# Enable versioning
resource "aws_s3_bucket_versioning" "data" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.data.id
  
  versioning_configuration {
    status = "Enabled"  # "Enabled" | "Suspended" | "Disabled"
  }
}

25.1.4 Encryption

hcl S3 Server-Side Encryption 
# SSE-S3 (Amazon managed keys)
resource "aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration" "data" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.data.id
  
  rule {
    apply_server_side_encryption_by_default {
      sse_algorithm = "AES256"
    }
    bucket_key_enabled = true
  }
}

# SSE-KMS (Customer managed keys)
resource "aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration" "secure" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.secure.id
  
  rule {
    apply_server_side_encryption_by_default {
      sse_algorithm     = "aws:kms"
      kms_master_key_id = aws_kms_key.s3.arn
    }
    bucket_key_enabled = true  # Reduces KMS costs
  }
}

25.1.5 Lifecycle Policies

hcl Lifecycle Configuration 
resource "aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration" "data" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.data.id
  
  # Must have versioning for noncurrent rules
  depends_on = [aws_s3_bucket_versioning.data]
  
  rule {
    id     = "transition-to-ia"
    status = "Enabled"
    
    filter {
      prefix = "logs/"
    }
    
    # Transition current objects
    transition {
      days          = 30
      storage_class = "STANDARD_IA"
    }
    
    transition {
      days          = 90
      storage_class = "GLACIER"
    }
    
    # Delete after 365 days
    expiration {
      days = 365
    }
    
    # Handle old versions
    noncurrent_version_transition {
      noncurrent_days = 30
      storage_class   = "GLACIER"
    }
    
    noncurrent_version_expiration {
      noncurrent_days = 90
    }
  }
  
  rule {
    id     = "abort-incomplete-uploads"
    status = "Enabled"
    
    filter {}  # Apply to all objects
    
    abort_incomplete_multipart_upload {
      days_after_initiation = 7
    }
  }
}
25.2

S3 Objects (aws_s3_object)

25.2.1-4 Object Upload and Storage Classes

hcl S3 Object Upload Methods 
# Upload from file
resource "aws_s3_object" "config" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.data.id
  key    = "config/app.json"
  source = "${path.module}/files/app.json"
  etag   = filemd5("${path.module}/files/app.json")
  
  content_type = "application/json"
  
  tags = {
    Name = "Application Config"
  }
}

# Upload from content (inline)
resource "aws_s3_object" "robots" {
  bucket       = aws_s3_bucket.website.id
  key          = "robots.txt"
  content      = "User-agent: *\nDisallow: /admin/"
  content_type = "text/plain"
}

# Templated content
resource "aws_s3_object" "env" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.data.id
  key    = "config/.env"
  content = templatefile("${path.module}/templates/env.tpl", {
    database_url = aws_rds_cluster.main.endpoint
    redis_url    = aws_elasticache_cluster.main.cache_nodes[0].address
  })
}

# With storage class
resource "aws_s3_object" "archive" {
  bucket        = aws_s3_bucket.data.id
  key           = "archives/old-data.tar.gz"
  source        = "${path.module}/archives/old-data.tar.gz"
  storage_class = "GLACIER"  # STANDARD, STANDARD_IA, GLACIER, DEEP_ARCHIVE
}
25.3

Static Website Hosting

25.3.1-4 Website Configuration

hcl S3 Static Website Hosting 
# S3 bucket for website
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "website" {
  bucket = "${var.project}-website"
}

# Website configuration
resource "aws_s3_bucket_website_configuration" "website" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.website.id
  
  index_document {
    suffix = "index.html"
  }
  
  error_document {
    key = "error.html"
  }
  
  routing_rule {
    condition {
      key_prefix_equals = "docs/"
    }
    redirect {
      replace_key_prefix_with = "documentation/"
    }
  }
}

# Public access for website
resource "aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block" "website" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.website.id
  
  block_public_acls       = false
  block_public_policy     = false
  ignore_public_acls      = false
  restrict_public_buckets = false
}

# Bucket policy for public read
resource "aws_s3_bucket_policy" "website" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.website.id
  
  depends_on = [aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block.website]
  
  policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Sid       = "PublicReadGetObject"
      Effect    = "Allow"
      Principal = "*"
      Action    = "s3:GetObject"
      Resource  = "${aws_s3_bucket.website.arn}/*"
    }]
  })
}

# Output website URL
output "website_url" {
  value = aws_s3_bucket_website_configuration.website.website_endpoint
}
25.4

Cross-Region Replication

25.4.1-2 CRR Configuration

hcl S3 Cross-Region Replication 
# Source bucket (us-east-1)
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "source" {
  provider = aws.east
  bucket   = "${var.project}-source"
}

resource "aws_s3_bucket_versioning" "source" {
  provider = aws.east
  bucket   = aws_s3_bucket.source.id
  versioning_configuration {
    status = "Enabled"
  }
}

# Destination bucket (us-west-2)
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "destination" {
  provider = aws.west
  bucket   = "${var.project}-replica"
}

resource "aws_s3_bucket_versioning" "destination" {
  provider = aws.west
  bucket   = aws_s3_bucket.destination.id
  versioning_configuration {
    status = "Enabled"
  }
}

# Replication role
resource "aws_iam_role" "replication" {
  name = "${var.project}-s3-replication-role"
  
  assume_role_policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Action = "sts:AssumeRole"
      Effect = "Allow"
      Principal = {
        Service = "s3.amazonaws.com"
      }
    }]
  })
}

# Replication configuration
resource "aws_s3_bucket_replication_configuration" "replication" {
  provider = aws.east
  bucket   = aws_s3_bucket.source.id
  role     = aws_iam_role.replication.arn
  
  depends_on = [aws_s3_bucket_versioning.source]
  
  rule {
    id     = "replicate-all"
    status = "Enabled"
    
    filter {}  # Replicate all objects
    
    destination {
      bucket        = aws_s3_bucket.destination.arn
      storage_class = "STANDARD"
      
      encryption_configuration {
        replica_kms_key_id = aws_kms_key.replica.arn
      }
    }
    
    delete_marker_replication {
      status = "Enabled"
    }
  }
}
25.5

Elastic File System (EFS)

25.5.1-4 EFS Configuration and Mount Targets

hcl Complete EFS Setup 
# EFS File System
resource "aws_efs_file_system" "shared" {
  creation_token = "${var.project}-efs"
  encrypted      = true
  kms_key_id     = aws_kms_key.efs.arn
  
  performance_mode                = "generalPurpose"  # or "maxIO"
  throughput_mode                 = "bursting"        # or "provisioned"
  # provisioned_throughput_in_mibps = 100  # If using provisioned
  
  lifecycle_policy {
    transition_to_ia = "AFTER_30_DAYS"
  }
  
  lifecycle_policy {
    transition_to_primary_storage_class = "AFTER_1_ACCESS"
  }
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-efs"
  }
}

# Mount targets (one per AZ)
resource "aws_efs_mount_target" "shared" {
  count = length(aws_subnet.private)
  
  file_system_id  = aws_efs_file_system.shared.id
  subnet_id       = aws_subnet.private[count.index].id
  security_groups = [aws_security_group.efs.id]
}

# Security group for EFS
resource "aws_security_group" "efs" {
  name        = "${var.project}-efs-sg"
  description = "Security group for EFS mount targets"
  vpc_id      = aws_vpc.main.id
  
  ingress {
    from_port       = 2049
    to_port         = 2049
    protocol        = "tcp"
    security_groups = [aws_security_group.web.id]
  }
  
  egress {
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }
}

# Access point (for containerized apps)
resource "aws_efs_access_point" "app" {
  file_system_id = aws_efs_file_system.shared.id
  
  posix_user {
    gid = 1000
    uid = 1000
  }
  
  root_directory {
    path = "/app"
    creation_info {
      owner_gid   = 1000
      owner_uid   = 1000
      permissions = "755"
    }
  }
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-app-access-point"
  }
}
25.6

EBS Snapshots and Backup

25.6.1-2 Snapshots and DLM Policies

hcl EBS Snapshots and Lifecycle Management 
# Manual snapshot
resource "aws_ebs_snapshot" "data" {
  volume_id = aws_ebs_volume.data.id
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-data-snapshot"
  }
}

# Data Lifecycle Manager (DLM) policy for automated snapshots
resource "aws_dlm_lifecycle_policy" "backup" {
  description        = "Daily EBS snapshots with 7-day retention"
  execution_role_arn = aws_iam_role.dlm.arn
  state              = "ENABLED"
  
  policy_details {
    resource_types = ["VOLUME"]
    
    target_tags = {
      Backup = "true"
    }
    
    schedule {
      name = "daily-snapshots"
      
      create_rule {
        interval      = 24
        interval_unit = "HOURS"
        times         = ["03:00"]
      }
      
      retain_rule {
        count = 7
      }
      
      tags_to_add = {
        SnapshotCreator = "DLM"
      }
      
      copy_tags = true
    }
  }
}

# DLM execution role
resource "aws_iam_role" "dlm" {
  name = "${var.project}-dlm-role"
  
  assume_role_policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Action = "sts:AssumeRole"
      Effect = "Allow"
      Principal = {
        Service = "dlm.amazonaws.com"
      }
    }]
  })
}

resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "dlm" {
  role       = aws_iam_role.dlm.name
  policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSDataLifecycleManagerServiceRole"
}

Chapter 25 Summary: AWS Storage Resources

Key Takeaways

  • AWS Provider 4.0+: S3 bucket settings are now separate resources (versioning, encryption, lifecycle)
  • Public Access Block: Always use aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block to prevent accidental public exposure
  • Lifecycle Policies: Transition objects between storage classes and expire old versions automatically
  • SSE-S3 vs SSE-KMS: SSE-S3 (AES256) is simpler; SSE-KMS provides key rotation and audit trails
  • Cross-Region Replication: Requires versioning enabled on both source and destination buckets
  • EFS: NFSv4 shared filesystem; create mount targets in each AZ for high availability
  • EFS Access Points: Enforce POSIX user/group and root directory for containerized applications
  • DLM: Use Data Lifecycle Manager for automated EBS snapshot schedules
Chapter 26

AWS Database Resources

RDS, Aurora, DynamoDB, and ElastiCache with Terraform

6 Sections
Part 8: AWS Resources
60 Minutes

Learning Objectives

🗄️

RDS Instances

Configure RDS with Multi-AZ, read replicas, and parameter groups

🌟

Aurora

Deploy Aurora clusters with Serverless v2 and Global Databases

⚡

DynamoDB

Create tables with GSIs, LSIs, and choose between On-Demand and Provisioned

🚀

ElastiCache

Configure Redis and Memcached clusters for caching

26.1

RDS Instances (aws_db_instance)

EXAM TOPIC
RDS Key Concepts: Multi-AZ provides automatic failover (synchronous replication). Read Replicas are for read scaling (asynchronous replication). final_snapshot_identifier or skip_final_snapshot = true is required for deletion.

26.1.1-2 RDS Instance Creation

hcl Complete RDS Instance 
# RDS PostgreSQL Instance
resource "aws_db_instance" "main" {
  identifier = "${var.project}-db"
  
  # Engine configuration
  engine               = "postgres"
  engine_version       = "15.4"
  instance_class       = "db.t3.micro"
  
  # Storage
  allocated_storage     = 20
  max_allocated_storage = 100    # Autoscaling
  storage_type          = "gp3"
  storage_encrypted     = true
  kms_key_id            = aws_kms_key.rds.arn
  
  # Database settings
  db_name  = "myapp"
  username = "admin"
  password = var.db_password
  port     = 5432
  
  # Networking
  db_subnet_group_name   = aws_db_subnet_group.main.name
  vpc_security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.rds.id]
  publicly_accessible    = false
  
  # High availability
  multi_az = var.environment == "prod"
  
  # Backup
  backup_retention_period = 7
  backup_window           = "03:00-04:00"
  maintenance_window      = "Mon:04:00-Mon:05:00"
  
  # Snapshots
  skip_final_snapshot       = var.environment != "prod"
  final_snapshot_identifier = "${var.project}-final-snapshot"
  copy_tags_to_snapshot     = true
  
  # Deletion protection
  deletion_protection = var.environment == "prod"
  
  # Performance Insights
  performance_insights_enabled          = true
  performance_insights_retention_period = 7
  
  # Monitoring
  monitoring_interval = 60
  monitoring_role_arn = aws_iam_role.rds_monitoring.arn
  
  # Parameter and option groups
  parameter_group_name = aws_db_parameter_group.main.name
  
  # Apply changes immediately (be careful in production)
  apply_immediately = var.environment != "prod"
  
  tags = {
    Name        = "${var.project}-db"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

26.1.3-4 Instance Classes and Storage Types

Instance Class Use Case vCPUs Memory
db.t3/t4g Development, burstable 2-8 1-32 GiB
db.r6g/r6i Memory-optimized production 2-96 16-768 GiB
db.m6g/m6i General purpose production 2-96 8-384 GiB

26.1.5-6 Multi-AZ and Read Replicas

hcl Read Replica Configuration 
# Read replica in same region
resource "aws_db_instance" "replica" {
  identifier     = "${var.project}-db-replica"
  instance_class = "db.t3.medium"
  
  # Reference the primary instance
  replicate_source_db = aws_db_instance.main.identifier
  
  # Replica-specific settings
  publicly_accessible    = false
  vpc_security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.rds.id]
  
  # Can have different parameter group
  parameter_group_name = aws_db_parameter_group.replica.name
  
  # Replica does not need backup
  backup_retention_period = 0
  skip_final_snapshot     = true
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-db-replica"
  }
}

# Cross-region read replica
resource "aws_db_instance" "cross_region_replica" {
  provider = aws.west
  
  identifier     = "${var.project}-db-replica-west"
  instance_class = "db.t3.medium"
  
  # Reference source by ARN for cross-region
  replicate_source_db = aws_db_instance.main.arn
  
  # Must specify in target region
  db_subnet_group_name   = aws_db_subnet_group.west.name
  vpc_security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.rds_west.id]
  
  storage_encrypted = true
  kms_key_id        = aws_kms_key.rds_west.arn
}
26.2

RDS Configuration Resources

26.2.1-3 Parameter Group, Option Group, Subnet Group

hcl RDS Configuration Resources 
# Parameter Group (database tuning)
resource "aws_db_parameter_group" "main" {
  name   = "${var.project}-pg15-params"
  family = "postgres15"
  
  parameter {
    name         = "log_connections"
    value        = "1"
    apply_method = "immediate"
  }
  
  parameter {
    name         = "log_disconnections"
    value        = "1"
    apply_method = "immediate"
  }
  
  parameter {
    name         = "shared_preload_libraries"
    value        = "pg_stat_statements"
    apply_method = "pending-reboot"
  }
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-parameters"
  }
}

# Option Group (Oracle/SQL Server features)
resource "aws_db_option_group" "mysql" {
  name                 = "${var.project}-mysql-options"
  engine_name          = "mysql"
  major_engine_version = "8.0"
  
  option {
    option_name = "MARIADB_AUDIT_PLUGIN"
  }
}

# Subnet Group (required for VPC)
resource "aws_db_subnet_group" "main" {
  name       = "${var.project}-db-subnet"
  subnet_ids = aws_subnet.private[*].id
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-db-subnet"
  }
}
26.3

Aurora

EXAM TOPIC
Aurora Architecture: Aurora uses aws_rds_cluster (the cluster) plus aws_rds_cluster_instance (the instances). The cluster provides reader and writer endpoints. Aurora Serverless v2 scales automatically from 0.5 to 128 ACUs.

26.3.1-2 Aurora Cluster and Instances

hcl Aurora PostgreSQL Cluster 
# Aurora Cluster
resource "aws_rds_cluster" "aurora" {
  cluster_identifier = "${var.project}-aurora"
  
  engine         = "aurora-postgresql"
  engine_version = "15.4"
  engine_mode    = "provisioned"  # or "serverless" for v1
  
  database_name   = "myapp"
  master_username = "admin"
  master_password = var.db_password
  port            = 5432
  
  # Networking
  db_subnet_group_name   = aws_db_subnet_group.main.name
  vpc_security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.aurora.id]
  
  # Storage
  storage_encrypted = true
  kms_key_id        = aws_kms_key.aurora.arn
  
  # Backup
  backup_retention_period = 7
  preferred_backup_window = "03:00-04:00"
  
  # Maintenance
  preferred_maintenance_window = "Mon:04:00-Mon:05:00"
  
  # Deletion
  skip_final_snapshot       = var.environment != "prod"
  final_snapshot_identifier = "${var.project}-aurora-final"
  deletion_protection       = var.environment == "prod"
  
  # Logs
  enabled_cloudwatch_logs_exports = ["postgresql"]
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-aurora"
  }
}

# Aurora Instances (minimum 1 writer required)
resource "aws_rds_cluster_instance" "aurora" {
  count = 2
  
  identifier         = "${var.project}-aurora-${count.index}"
  cluster_identifier = aws_rds_cluster.aurora.id
  
  instance_class = "db.r6g.large"
  engine         = aws_rds_cluster.aurora.engine
  engine_version = aws_rds_cluster.aurora.engine_version
  
  publicly_accessible = false
  
  # Performance Insights
  performance_insights_enabled = true
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-aurora-${count.index}"
  }
}

26.3.3 Aurora Serverless v2

hcl Aurora Serverless v2 
# Aurora Serverless v2 Cluster
resource "aws_rds_cluster" "serverless" {
  cluster_identifier = "${var.project}-aurora-serverless"
  
  engine         = "aurora-postgresql"
  engine_version = "15.4"
  engine_mode    = "provisioned"  # v2 uses provisioned mode
  
  database_name   = "myapp"
  master_username = "admin"
  master_password = var.db_password
  
  db_subnet_group_name   = aws_db_subnet_group.main.name
  vpc_security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.aurora.id]
  
  storage_encrypted = true
  
  # Serverless v2 scaling configuration
  serverlessv2_scaling_configuration {
    min_capacity = 0.5   # Minimum ACUs
    max_capacity = 16    # Maximum ACUs
  }
  
  skip_final_snapshot = true
}

# Serverless v2 instance
resource "aws_rds_cluster_instance" "serverless" {
  cluster_identifier = aws_rds_cluster.serverless.id
  identifier         = "${var.project}-aurora-serverless-1"
  
  instance_class = "db.serverless"  # Required for Serverless v2
  engine         = aws_rds_cluster.serverless.engine
  engine_version = aws_rds_cluster.serverless.engine_version
}

26.3.4 Aurora Global Database

hcl Aurora Global Database 
# Global Database
resource "aws_rds_global_cluster" "global" {
  global_cluster_identifier = "${var.project}-global"
  engine                    = "aurora-postgresql"
  engine_version            = "15.4"
  database_name             = "myapp"
  storage_encrypted         = true
}

# Primary cluster (us-east-1)
resource "aws_rds_cluster" "primary" {
  provider = aws.east
  
  cluster_identifier        = "${var.project}-primary"
  global_cluster_identifier = aws_rds_global_cluster.global.id
  
  engine         = aws_rds_global_cluster.global.engine
  engine_version = aws_rds_global_cluster.global.engine_version
  
  master_username = "admin"
  master_password = var.db_password
  
  db_subnet_group_name   = aws_db_subnet_group.east.name
  vpc_security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.aurora_east.id]
}

# Secondary cluster (us-west-2)
resource "aws_rds_cluster" "secondary" {
  provider = aws.west
  
  cluster_identifier        = "${var.project}-secondary"
  global_cluster_identifier = aws_rds_global_cluster.global.id
  
  engine         = aws_rds_global_cluster.global.engine
  engine_version = aws_rds_global_cluster.global.engine_version
  
  # No master credentials for secondary
  
  db_subnet_group_name   = aws_db_subnet_group.west.name
  vpc_security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.aurora_west.id]
  
  depends_on = [aws_rds_cluster.primary]
}
26.4

DynamoDB (aws_dynamodb_table)

EXAM TOPIC
DynamoDB Keys: Hash key (partition key) is required. Range key (sort key) is optional. GSI has its own partition/sort keys and can be added anytime. LSI must be created at table creation time and shares the table's partition key.

26.4.1-2 Table with Primary Key

hcl DynamoDB Table Configuration 
# DynamoDB Table with composite key
resource "aws_dynamodb_table" "orders" {
  name         = "${var.project}-orders"
  billing_mode = "PAY_PER_REQUEST"  # On-demand
  
  # Primary key
  hash_key  = "customer_id"   # Partition key
  range_key = "order_id"      # Sort key
  
  # Attribute definitions (only for keys/indexes)
  attribute {
    name = "customer_id"
    type = "S"  # S=String, N=Number, B=Binary
  }
  
  attribute {
    name = "order_id"
    type = "S"
  }
  
  attribute {
    name = "order_date"
    type = "S"
  }
  
  attribute {
    name = "status"
    type = "S"
  }
  
  # Global Secondary Index
  global_secondary_index {
    name            = "status-date-index"
    hash_key        = "status"
    range_key       = "order_date"
    projection_type = "ALL"  # ALL, KEYS_ONLY, or INCLUDE
  }
  
  # Local Secondary Index (must be created at table creation)
  local_secondary_index {
    name            = "customer-date-index"
    range_key       = "order_date"
    projection_type = "ALL"
  }
  
  # Point-in-time recovery
  point_in_time_recovery {
    enabled = true
  }
  
  # Server-side encryption
  server_side_encryption {
    enabled     = true
    kms_key_arn = aws_kms_key.dynamodb.arn
  }
  
  # TTL
  ttl {
    attribute_name = "expires_at"
    enabled        = true
  }
  
  # DynamoDB Streams
  stream_enabled   = true
  stream_view_type = "NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES"  # KEYS_ONLY, NEW_IMAGE, OLD_IMAGE, NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-orders"
  }
}

26.4.5 On-Demand vs Provisioned Capacity

hcl Provisioned Capacity with Auto Scaling 
# Provisioned capacity table
resource "aws_dynamodb_table" "sessions" {
  name         = "${var.project}-sessions"
  billing_mode = "PROVISIONED"
  
  hash_key = "session_id"
  
  attribute {
    name = "session_id"
    type = "S"
  }
  
  # Base capacity
  read_capacity  = 5
  write_capacity = 5
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-sessions"
  }
}

# Auto scaling for read capacity
resource "aws_appautoscaling_target" "read" {
  max_capacity       = 100
  min_capacity       = 5
  resource_id        = "table/${aws_dynamodb_table.sessions.name}"
  scalable_dimension = "dynamodb:table:ReadCapacityUnits"
  service_namespace  = "dynamodb"
}

resource "aws_appautoscaling_policy" "read" {
  name               = "${var.project}-read-scaling"
  policy_type        = "TargetTrackingScaling"
  resource_id        = aws_appautoscaling_target.read.resource_id
  scalable_dimension = aws_appautoscaling_target.read.scalable_dimension
  service_namespace  = aws_appautoscaling_target.read.service_namespace
  
  target_tracking_scaling_policy_configuration {
    predefined_metric_specification {
      predefined_metric_type = "DynamoDBReadCapacityUtilization"
    }
    target_value = 70.0
  }
}
Capacity Mode Best For Pricing
On-Demand Unpredictable/variable traffic Pay per request
Provisioned Predictable, steady traffic Pay per capacity unit/hour
26.5

ElastiCache

26.5.1-2 ElastiCache Redis Cluster

hcl Redis Replication Group (Recommended) 
# Subnet group for ElastiCache
resource "aws_elasticache_subnet_group" "redis" {
  name       = "${var.project}-redis-subnet"
  subnet_ids = aws_subnet.private[*].id
}

# Parameter group
resource "aws_elasticache_parameter_group" "redis" {
  name   = "${var.project}-redis-params"
  family = "redis7"
  
  parameter {
    name  = "maxmemory-policy"
    value = "allkeys-lru"
  }
}

# Redis Replication Group (HA setup)
resource "aws_elasticache_replication_group" "redis" {
  replication_group_id = "${var.project}-redis"
  description         = "Redis cluster for ${var.project}"
  
  node_type            = "cache.t3.micro"
  engine_version       = "7.0"
  port                 = 6379
  parameter_group_name = aws_elasticache_parameter_group.redis.name
  
  # Cluster mode disabled (single shard)
  num_cache_clusters = 2  # 1 primary + 1 replica
  
  # Networking
  subnet_group_name  = aws_elasticache_subnet_group.redis.name
  security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.redis.id]
  
  # Encryption
  at_rest_encryption_enabled = true
  transit_encryption_enabled = true
  auth_token                 = var.redis_auth_token
  
  # Automatic failover (requires num_cache_clusters >= 2)
  automatic_failover_enabled = true
  multi_az_enabled           = true
  
  # Maintenance
  maintenance_window       = "Mon:03:00-Mon:04:00"
  snapshot_window          = "02:00-03:00"
  snapshot_retention_limit = 7
  
  # Apply changes
  apply_immediately = var.environment != "prod"
  
  tags = {
    Name = "${var.project}-redis"
  }
}

26.5.3-4 Redis vs Memcached and Cluster Mode

Feature Redis Memcached
Data structures Strings, Lists, Sets, Hashes Simple key-value only
Persistence Yes (RDB/AOF) No
Replication Yes (read replicas) No
Cluster mode Yes (sharding) Manual sharding
Multi-threaded No (single-threaded) Yes
hcl Redis Cluster Mode Enabled 
# Cluster mode enabled (sharding)
resource "aws_elasticache_replication_group" "redis_cluster" {
  replication_group_id = "${var.project}-redis-cluster"
  description          = "Redis cluster with sharding"
  
  node_type       = "cache.r6g.large"
  engine_version  = "7.0"
  
  # Cluster mode enabled
  num_node_groups         = 3        # Number of shards
  replicas_per_node_group = 1        # Replicas per shard
  
  automatic_failover_enabled = true
  multi_az_enabled           = true
  
  subnet_group_name  = aws_elasticache_subnet_group.redis.name
  security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.redis.id]
}
26.6

Other Database Services

26.6.1-4 DocumentDB, Neptune, Redshift, Timestream

hcl Specialized Database Services 
# DocumentDB (MongoDB compatible)
resource "aws_docdb_cluster" "mongodb" {
  cluster_identifier  = "${var.project}-docdb"
  engine              = "docdb"
  master_username     = "admin"
  master_password     = var.docdb_password
  db_subnet_group_name = aws_docdb_subnet_group.main.name
  vpc_security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.docdb.id]
  storage_encrypted   = true
}

resource "aws_docdb_cluster_instance" "mongodb" {
  count              = 2
  identifier         = "${var.project}-docdb-${count.index}"
  cluster_identifier = aws_docdb_cluster.mongodb.id
  instance_class     = "db.r6g.large"
}

# Neptune (Graph database)
resource "aws_neptune_cluster" "graph" {
  cluster_identifier = "${var.project}-neptune"
  engine             = "neptune"
  neptune_subnet_group_name = aws_neptune_subnet_group.main.name
  vpc_security_group_ids    = [aws_security_group.neptune.id]
  storage_encrypted  = true
}

# Redshift (Data warehouse)
resource "aws_redshift_cluster" "warehouse" {
  cluster_identifier = "${var.project}-redshift"
  database_name      = "warehouse"
  master_username    = "admin"
  master_password    = var.redshift_password
  node_type          = "dc2.large"
  cluster_type       = "multi-node"
  number_of_nodes    = 2
  encrypted          = true
}

# Timestream (Time series)
resource "aws_timestreamwrite_database" "timeseries" {
  database_name = "${var.project}-timeseries"
}

resource "aws_timestreamwrite_table" "metrics" {
  database_name = aws_timestreamwrite_database.timeseries.database_name
  table_name    = "metrics"
  
  retention_properties {
    memory_store_retention_period_in_hours  = 24
    magnetic_store_retention_period_in_days = 365
  }
}

Chapter 26 Summary: AWS Database Resources

Key Takeaways

  • RDS Multi-AZ: Synchronous replication for automatic failover; Read Replicas use async replication for scaling
  • RDS Deletion: Must specify final_snapshot_identifier or skip_final_snapshot = true
  • Aurora: Uses aws_rds_cluster + aws_rds_cluster_instance; provides reader and writer endpoints
  • Aurora Serverless v2: Use instance_class = "db.serverless" and scaling configuration
  • DynamoDB Keys: Hash key (partition) required; Range key (sort) optional; LSI created at table creation only
  • DynamoDB Capacity: PAY_PER_REQUEST for variable traffic; PROVISIONED with auto scaling for predictable traffic
  • ElastiCache: Use aws_elasticache_replication_group for HA; Redis supports persistence and replication, Memcached does not
  • Redis Cluster Mode: Enable for horizontal scaling with sharding (num_node_groups)
Chapter 27

AWS IAM Resources

Users, Groups, Roles, Policies, and OIDC with Terraform

6 Sections
Part 8: AWS Resources
50 Minutes

Learning Objectives

👤

IAM Users & Groups

Create users, groups, and manage memberships

🎭

IAM Roles

Create roles with assume role policies and trust relationships

📋

IAM Policies

Write policies with aws_iam_policy_document data source

🔐

OIDC

Configure OIDC providers for GitHub Actions and EKS

27.1

IAM Users

27.1.1-4 User Creation and Policy Attachment

hcl IAM User Management 
# IAM User
resource "aws_iam_user" "developer" {
  name          = "developer"
  path          = "/developers/"
  force_destroy = true  # Delete even if user has non-Terraform resources
  
  tags = {
    Team = "Engineering"
  }
}

# Inline policy (attached directly to user)
resource "aws_iam_user_policy" "developer_s3" {
  name = "developer-s3-access"
  user = aws_iam_user.developer.name
  
  policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Effect   = "Allow"
      Action   = ["s3:GetObject", "s3:PutObject"]
      Resource = "${aws_s3_bucket.dev.arn}/*"
    }]
  })
}

# Attach managed policy to user
resource "aws_iam_user_policy_attachment" "developer_readonly" {
  user       = aws_iam_user.developer.name
  policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/ReadOnlyAccess"
}

# Create multiple users with for_each
resource "aws_iam_user" "developers" {
  for_each = toset(["alice", "bob", "carol"])
  
  name = each.key
  path = "/developers/"
}

# Access key for programmatic access
resource "aws_iam_access_key" "developer" {
  user = aws_iam_user.developer.name
}

# Output credentials (be careful!)
